title: rhinanthus grandiflorus
intro: Rhinanthus grandiflorus, also known as the large yellow rattle or devil's snare, is a plant species that belongs to the family Orobanchaceae. It is a hemiparasitic plant that obtains some of its nutrients by connecting to the roots of other plants, typically grasses and wildflowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its expectorant and antitussive properties, which help to relieve coughs and congestion. The health benefits of Rhinanthus grandiflorus include reducing inflammation and promoting respiratory health, and it can be prepared as a tea or tincture for medicinal use.
